user_query:
Please set up my vehicle maintenance tracker and trip prep using the provided files, and write all artifacts to output/.

Read these files under input/:
- input/vehicles.json — vehicles to track (two entries: my 2019 F-150 and my 2018 R-Pod travel trailer) with VINs, current mileage, and warranty details
- input/fuel_logs.csv — recent fill-ups for the F-150
- input/settings.yaml — assumed "today" date, plus thresholds for warranty alerts
- input/trip_plan.txt — we’re towing the R-Pod from Phoenix to Flagstaff this weekend; use this for a pre-tow checklist

Then complete all of the following:

1) Build a consolidated state file at output/data/mechanic/state.json
- Include a top-level vehicles object with keys exactly matching the slugs from input/vehicles.json (expecting: f150 and rpod_trailer)
- For each vehicle:
  - Set label and schedule_file (e.g., f150-schedule.json, rpod_trailer-schedule.json)
  - Store the VIN in vin. If you can’t reach external services, set vin_data.decoded = false and leave other vin_data fields blank/omitted (do not block the workflow)
  - Set current_miles from input and append a mileage_history entry with date = settings.yaml: today and miles = current_miles
  - Copy warranties from input verbatim into warranties[]
  - Initialize recalls with last_checked = null and empty arrays for open_recalls and completed_recalls
- For the F-150 only, ingest input/fuel_logs.csv into fuel_history[] entries with fields: date, gallons, cost, odometer, partial_fill (boolean), and computed mpg per fill-up using this exact rule:
  - Compute per-fill mpg as (current odometer – previous non-partial fill’s odometer) / gallons for the current fill-up
  - Skip MPG computation for any fill marked partial_fill = true and do not use partial fills as the "previous" for MPG calculations

2) Create per-vehicle service schedule files under output/data/mechanic/
- output/data/mechanic/f150-schedule.json — at least 5 services, each with required fields: id, name, details, priority, cost_diy, cost_shop, cost_dealer, and at least one interval (interval_miles or interval_months). This file must include a service with id = "oil_filter"
- output/data/mechanic/rpod_trailer-schedule.json — at least 3 services with the same required fields. This file must include a service with id = "wheel_bearing_repack"
- In each schedule file, include a vehicle section with: year, make, model, type, transmission/engine where applicable, and duty level (choose sensible defaults; you may derive from input/settings.yaml if provided, otherwise set "duty": "normal")

3) Fuel economy report for the F-150
- Write output/reports/fuel_report_f150.json with fields: last_fill_date, last_mpg (number), rolling_avg (number), drop_percent (number), anomaly (boolean)
- Compute rolling_avg as the average MPG of the three most recent completed (non-partial) fill-ups immediately BEFORE the most recent non-partial fill-up
- Compute drop_percent = ((rolling_avg - last_mpg) / rolling_avg) * 100 (use positive percentage for a drop)
- Set anomaly = true if last_mpg is more than 15% below rolling_avg; otherwise false

4) Warranty alerts report
- Using settings.yaml: months_threshold and miles_threshold, write output/reports/warranty_alerts.md listing any warranties that are expiring soon (within months_threshold or within miles_threshold of end_miles) based on today from settings.yaml
- For each expiring-soon warranty, include: vehicle label, warranty type, provider, end_date, end_miles if applicable, and a clear note that it is "expiring soon"

5) Pre-tow checklist for this weekend’s Phoenix→Flagstaff trip
- Write output/reports/pre_tow_checklist.md combining a general towing checklist with truck+trailer specifics, and include at minimum these three section headings exactly:
  - TRUCK:
  - HITCH/CONNECTION:
  - TRAILER/RV:
- Each section should contain multiple checkbox items using the "□ " prefix (e.g., "□ Tire pressures set to towing spec")
- Tailor the checklist to the described mountain/altitude route and towing context in input/trip_plan.txt

Notes and constraints:
- Only read from input/ paths and only write to output/ paths
- Use valid JSON for all .json outputs and ensure schedule_file paths in state.json point to the files you created
- Do not use absolute machine paths or rely on external APIs; if VIN decoding or recall checks aren’t reachable, leave vin_data.decoded = false and recalls.last_checked = null
- Keep filenames and paths exactly as specified so automated checks can find them
